title: selinum carvifolia subsp. broteroi
intro: Selinum carvifolia subsp. broteroi, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate digestive issues and reduce pain. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, lowering cholesterol levels, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The leaves and roots of Selinum carvifolia subsp. broteroi can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps.
